rawCommits.subject: fix(server): preserve org identity for remote chat
rawCommits.body:
Populate chat session org identity from the project when agent metadata is incomplete so remote harness sessions carry the required X-Aura-Org-Id context.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: preserve org identity for remote chat

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): make Windows updater handoff observable
rawCommits.body:
Capture installer handoff logs and relaunch Aura after the NSIS installer completes so Windows updates do not silently quit without diagnostics.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(server): fail remote chat streams when harness stalls
rawCommits.body:
Make Swarm session startup wait for session_ready and broadcast explicit errors when remote streams go silent or emit unsupported websocket frames, so chat does not remain stuck on connecting copy.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: fail remote chat streams when harness stalls

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): start updater installs off runtime thread
rawCommits.body:
Run the user-approved install on a named OS thread so the tao event loop does not need a Tokio reactor, and let the Windows smoke script read NSIS DisplayVersion when PE metadata is empty.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: start updater installs off runtime thread

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): avoid final assistant bubble remount
rawCommits.body:
Keep the tail assistant row stable when persisted history replaces the streaming placeholder so the end-of-turn UI does not flash.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): refocus standalone chat input
rawCommits.body:
Restore the shared desktop autofocus path when switching standalone agents so the chat composer is ready after the target thread loads.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: chore(build): clean up npm build warnings
rawCommits.body:
Replace ineffective dynamic imports with static store imports and align the Vite chunk warning limit with the current vendor split. Include outstanding Rust formatting cleanup.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): layer remote status hover above thinking indicator
rawCommits.body:
Portal the agent environment status card out of the chat input stacking context so it can render above streaming indicators while keeping existing interactions intact.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: layer remote status hover above thinking indicator

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): keep agent responses stable after streaming
rawCommits.body:
Preserve the streamed assistant tail until persisted history semantically catches up so agent chats do not blink or reload stale final content.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: keep agent responses stable after streaming

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): identify CEO agents explicitly
rawCommits.body:
Avoid treating full-access permissions as CEO identity so regular privileged agents keep their own role while the bootstrap CEO remains deduped by explicit identity.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): pass relaunch arg to Windows updater
rawCommits.body:
Align the custom NSIS handoff with cargo-packager-updater so the staged installer can run and relaunch Aura after shutdown.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: pass relaunch arg to Windows updater

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): stop stream history blink regression
rawCommits.body:
Restore the dedicated streaming bubble path, avoid no-op history rewrites, and recover empty terminal assistant rows from persisted deltas so cross-agent replies survive reloads.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): stop transcript flash on history flap mid-turn
rawCommits.body:
Latch the cold-load reveal in ChatPanel so a transient
`historyResolved=false` flip during a turn cannot re-arm
`initialColdLoadRef` and re-apply `.messageContentHidden` to the entire
transcript.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: stop transcript flash on history flap mid-turn

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): unify standalone agent chat wiring + stop CEO transcript blink
rawCommits.body:
Two coupled changes that together close the "CEO chat keeps blinking the
whole transcript mid-turn" bug:

1. Consolidate StandaloneAgentChatPanel (route view) and useAgentChatWindow
   (floating desktop window) onto a single shared hook
   `useStandaloneAgentChat(agentId)`. Both surfaces now route through the
   exact same wiring (useAgentChatStream + useChatHistorySync + project
   selection + context-usage hydration), so any future fix applies to
   both at once. The route panel layers only its route-specific
   side-effects (LAST_AGENT_ID_KEY persistence, `initialHandoff`,
   `scrollToBottomOnReset`) on top.

2. Add a per-streamKey "last non-empty" fallback inside
   `useConversationSnapshot`. Mid-turn, three things can collapse the
   merged `messages` to `[]` in the same render:
   - chat-history-store eviction (`MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES = 8`) flips
     `historyMessages` to `[]` while a sidebar prefetch displaces the
     active CEO key,
   - the post-stream "history caught up" effect in `useChatHistorySync`
     resets the stream-store events to `[]`,
   - `setThread(streamKey, history)` in `useConversationSnapshot` runs
     in a later effect tick so the message-store thread momentarily
     has nothing to fall back on.
   When all three line up, ChatMessageList renders its empty state and
   the entire transcript visibly disappears for one or two frames. The
   fallback caches the last non-empty merged snapshot keyed by
   `streamKey` and returns it across these transient empty frames.
   Resets cleanly on real chat switches; brand-new chats with no
   history still render as legitimately empty.

Tests:
- `use-conversation-snapshot.test.ts`: 3 new regression specs covering
  the empty-frame fallback, the brand-new-chat empty case, and the
  cache reset on streamKey change. Verified the empty-frame test fails
  without the fix.
- `use-standalone-agent-chat.test.ts`: full move of the prior
  `use-agent-chat-window.test.ts` suite onto the consolidated hook.
- `AgentChatView.test.tsx`: extends the existing standalone-agent
  assertions to cover the new `useStandaloneAgentMeta` /
  `useAgentStore` wiring on the route surface.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: unify standalone agent chat wiring + stop CEO transcript blink

rendered.title: Steadier chat transcripts and a recoverable Windows updater
rendered.intro: Today's nightly is a deep stabilization pass on the chat surface — taking aim at the mid-turn blink, transcript flashes, and CEO-chat regressions — paired with meaningful reliability work on the Windows auto-updater and remote agent streaming.

entries.title: Remote org identity and Windows updater diagnostics
entries.summary: Two reliability fixes: remote chat sessions now carry the right org context, and Windows updates surface logs and relaunch instead of silently quitting.

items.text: Remote harness chat sessions now derive their X-Aura-Org-Id from the project when agent metadata is incomplete, so server-side org context is preserved across remote turns.

Windows auto-update is no longer a black box: the NSIS installer handoff now writes a per-version log to the updater stage directory, and a single-instance retry loop keyed off AURA_UPDATE_RELAUNCH waits out the old process so Aura comes back up cleanly post-install.

entries.title: Stall detection for remote chat streams
entries.summary: Server-side watchdogs and explicit error broadcasts ensure remote chats fail visibly when the harness goes silent instead of sitting on a connecting spinner.

items.text: Swarm session startup now waits for an explicit session_ready signal before considering the remote agent live, eliminating a class of races where chats appeared connected but had no upstream.

A new turn watchdog broadcasts a synthetic terminal error if the remote runtime emits no events within 30s, or if the turn exceeds the 5-minute ceiling — so SSE keep-alives can no longer mask a stalled stream. Unsupported websocket frames now also surface as explicit errors.

entries.title: Updater install moved off the Tokio runtime thread
entries.summary: Desktop installs now run on a dedicated OS thread, and the local update smoke script is more tolerant of empty PE metadata.

items.text: User-approved updater installs now run on a named OS thread, so the tao event loop no longer needs a live Tokio reactor at install time — removing a deadlock vector in the Windows update path.
The desktop auto-update smoke script falls back to the NSIS DisplayVersion registry value when PE metadata is empty, making local update verification work against more installer builds.

entries.title: Stable assistant tails, sidebar previews, and CEO role handling
entries.summary: A broad polish pass on chat and agent UI: streaming bubbles stop remounting, sidebar previews stop thrashing, and CEO identity is no longer inferred from permissions.

items.text: The trailing assistant bubble now stays mounted as streaming text grows and as persisted history catches up, eliminating the end-of-turn flash in both project chats and standalone agent chats.
Agent sidebar previews stay stable across history-store updates, and switching between standalone agents once again hands focus back to the chat composer when the target thread is ready.

The remote agent environment status card is now portaled out of the chat input's stacking context and pinned via fixed positioning, so it can hover above the streaming/thinking indicator without losing hover interactions.

CEO identity is now resolved by the explicit bootstrap agent id rather than by matching the full-access permissions preset, so privileged non-CEO agents keep their own role and the bootstrap CEO is still deduped correctly. Hibernating swarm agents are also woken up reliably for new turns.

entries.title: Windows updater relaunch argument aligned with cargo-packager
entries.summary: A small but load-bearing fix that lets the staged NSIS installer actually relaunch Aura after shutdown.

items.text: The custom NSIS handoff now passes the relaunch argument expected by cargo-packager-updater, so the staged installer runs to completion and reopens Aura instead of leaving the user on a closed window.

entries.title: End of the CEO transcript blink
entries.summary: A coordinated front-end and server fix closes out the long-standing mid-turn transcript disappearance in CEO and standalone agent chats.

items.text: Empty terminal assistant rows are now reconstructed from persisted text and thinking deltas on reload, so cross-agent replies survive a refresh even when the persistence pipeline lagged on the original turn.

ChatPanel now latches its cold-load reveal so a transient historyResolved=false flip mid-turn can no longer re-hide the entire transcript, ending the full-transcript flash users were seeing during long replies.

The standalone agent route view and the floating desktop agent window now share a single useStandaloneAgentChat hook, and useConversationSnapshot caches the last non-empty merged snapshot per streamKey — together eliminating the one-to-two-frame empty render that caused the CEO transcript to blink mid-turn.

highlights: Stalled remote chats now fail loudly instead of hanging on 'connecting'
Windows updater handoff is observable and reliably relaunches Aura
Standalone agent route and floating window now share one chat wiring
